mnemonics: Desert ABCs: Arid, Barren, and Cacti (for remembering the core characteristics of a desert ecosystem)
HOT DIP: High temperatures, Organisms adapted, Thin soils, Dry, Intense sunlight, Precipitation low (for remembering key abiotic factors)
Not Just Sand: Deserts are diverse ecosystems that include rock, gravel, and cold climates (to counter the common misconception that all deserts are sandy and hot)
keyPoints: A desert is defined by its aridity (low precipitation), not its temperature. This includes cold polar deserts.
Desert ecosystems have highly specialized flora (e.g., succulents, deep roots) and fauna (e.g., nocturnal behavior, water conservation) adapted to extreme conditions.

A major environmental threat to deserts is improper e-waste disposal, as the dry climate slows decomposition and toxic heavy metals can leach into and contaminate the fragile soil and scarce water sources.
commonMistakes: Believing all deserts are hot; cold deserts (like the Gobi or Antarctica) are a major category.
Thinking deserts are barren wastelands with no biodiversity; they contain unique and highly adapted life forms.
Assuming e-waste is only an urban issue; illegal dumping in remote desert areas is a significant problem due to the vast, unmonitored space.
